blocks.text:
ทำไม neonatologist ต้องรู้ Cardiac Embryology?
CHD ประมาณ 8 ใน 1,000 live births. เข้าใจ embryology = เข้าใจ defect จาก root cause → predict associated anomalies + counsel families. (1) Heart tube formed by week 3; complete 4-chamber by week 9; (2) Secondary heart field → RV + outflow tract; defects = conotruncal anomalies; (3) Neural crest → septate great arteries; ablation → conotruncal defects; (4) DiGeorge syndrome = 22q11 deletion / TBX1 → conotruncal + arch defects + parathyroid/thymus/thyroid; (5) Heterotaxy = left-right axis genes (SHH, NODAL, PITX2) → randomized organ situs; (6) Noonan syndrome = PTPN11 gain-of-function → dysplastic pulmonary valve.
เป้าหมายการเรียนรู้วันนี้
อธิบาย cardiac developmental timeline: gastrulation → crescent → tube (wk 3) → looping (wk 4–5) → septation → complete heart (wk 7–9)
อธิบาย 4 cell populations: primary heart field, secondary heart field, neural crest cells, proepicardial organ
อธิบาย endocardial cushion formation + function: AV canal septation + conotruncal septation
รู้ clinical defects จาก conotruncal anomalies: TOF, truncus arteriosus, DORV, TGV, IAA
อธิบาย DiGeorge syndrome: 22q11 deletion, TBX1 gene, cardiac + extracardiac features
อธิบาย looping laterality + heterotaxy syndromes: right vs left isomerism, associated CHD
อธิบาย ventricular/atrial septal development + clinical defects (VSD, ASD types, AVSD)
อธิบาย aortic arch development: 6 arches, fate of each, clinical anomalies
อธิบาย ductus arteriosus embryology + clinical significance
อธิบาย coronary artery origins + conduction system development
1. Overview of Cardiac Development Timeline

rows: Cardiac mesoderm specified; cardiac crescent forms
Crescent fuses midline → linear heart tube; heart beats
Tube loops right (D-looping); left-right asymmetry established

blocks.text: 2. Cell Populations Contributing to the Heart
2.1 Primary (First) Heart Field
Lateral plate mesoderm → forms primitive heart tube. Gives rise to: left ventricle + most of atria + AV canal myocardium

Transcription factors: GATA4, NKX2.5, TBX5, Hand1. TBX5 + GATA4 → recruit to muscle-specific gene promoters. TBX5 haploinsufficiency → ASD + conduction defects + limb anomalies (Holt-Oram syndrome). NKX2.5 mutations → ASD + conduction system defects
2.2 Secondary (Anterior) Heart Field
Mesoderm adjacent to primary field; migrates into heart during looping. Contributes: entire RV, outflow tract, atrial and ventricular septa, parts of atria
Key transcription factors: TBX1, Islet1 (Isl1), Mef2c, FGF8, FGF10, TBX20. Knockout of any → outflow tract defects in mice

TBX1 = key for secondary heart field patterning; coordinates cell migration at both arterial and venous poles. TBX1 deficiency → conotruncal defects. TBX1 homozygous knockout in mice → 22q11.2 DiGeorge phenotype
2.3 Neural Crest Cells

From anterior rhombencephalon ectoderm → migrate through pharyngeal arches into conotruncus. Critical for: septation of great arteries (aorticopulmonary septum) + closure of conal septum + innervation of heart + smooth muscle of proximal aorta + development of parathyroid, thyroid, thymus
Neural crest ablation in chick → conotruncal defects + abnormal parathyroid/thymus/thyroid = DiGeorge phenotype
TBX1 not expressed in neural crest, but critical for neural crest-secondary heart field interactions. Most 22q11 deletions > 3 Mb; TBX1 single-gene mutations rare but sufficient to cause phenotype
2.4 Proepicardial Organ

Cluster of cells dorsal/inferior to heart tube; possibly derived from liver primordium. Spreads as epithelium → forms epicardium. Subgroup undergoes EMT → migrate into myocardium → differentiate into: coronary artery smooth muscle, cardiac fibroblasts; limited endothelial contribution (4%)
3. Gastrulation and Cardiac Crescent

Epiblast cells delaminate along primitive streak → invade between epiblast + hypoblast → cardiac crescent. Positional fate: apex of crescent = outflow tract myocytes (migrated through primitive node); mid = ventricular; lateral/caudal = atrial

Transcription factor cascade: Mesp1 → GATA4/TBX5/NKX2.5/Islet1. Wnt inhibition in crescent → BMP activation → cardiomyocyte differentiation. Wnt at primitive node → maintains proliferation/migration (undifferentiated state)

Left-right asymmetry set by sonic hedgehog (SHH) + FGF8 gradient at primitive node; ciliary currents → NODAL → LEFTY → PITX2 = left-sided markers. Right-sidedness = absence of these factors. Disruption → heterotaxy
4. Cardiac Looping and Laterality
4.1 Normal D-Looping

Symmetric heart tube → D-looping (rightward): sinus venosus + atria move left/posterior; ventricles + bulbus cordis move right/anterior. Bulbus cordis remains right of primitive ventricle. Normal cardiac connections: (S,D,S) — situs solitus, D-loop, normal great arteries
Secondary heart field migrates into both arterial and venous poles → 5× elongation of heart tube during looping. RV entirely from secondary heart field (lineage tracing in chick embryos)
4.2 Heterotaxy Syndromes

Heterotaxy — Left-Right Axis Randomization
Heterotaxy = abnormal L-R asymmetry; left-right axis randomized. Right isomerism (asplenia syndrome): bilateral right-sidedness; asplenia, bilateral trilobed lungs, bilateral SVC, AVSD, pulmonary atresia/stenosis, TAPVR, single ventricle. Left isomerism (polysplenia syndrome): bilateral left-sidedness; polysplenia, bilateral bilobed lungs, endocardial cushion defects, coarctation, IVC interruption with azygous continuation. Both: abnormal situs → pulmonary veins return ipsilaterally; systemic veins to either atrium.

Congenitally corrected transposition (S,L,L or I,D,D): atrioventricular + ventriculoarterial discordance → physiologically 'corrected' circulation but RV acts as systemic ventricle → eventual RV failure. Often has associated VSD + PS
5. Endocardial Cushion Formation
5.1 AV Canal Septation

Endocardial cushions = swellings ใน AV canal (week 6); endocardial cells undergo epithelial-to-mesenchymal transformation (EMT) → invade extracellular matrix → 4 cushions (superior, inferior, left lateral, right lateral)
Superior + inferior cushions fuse midline → separate AV canal into left (mitral) + right (tricuspid) sides. Mitral valve = 2 leaflets; tricuspid = 3 leaflets

Partial AVSD (primum ASD): incomplete cushion fusion → cleft anterior mitral leaflet + primum ASD. Complete AVSD: unformed crux of heart → common AV orifice + atrial + ventricular septal defects + 4–5 leaflet common AV valve
5.2 Conotruncal Septation

4 conotruncal cushions form 3D spiral → ~180° rotation to separate aorta from pulmonary artery. Truncal cushions → aorticopulmonary septum (+ neural crest); conal cushions → pulmonary + aortic valve formation
If 180° twist absent/incomplete → transposition of great arteries: aortic valve anterior to pulmonary valve

rows: Single outflow over both ventricles + outlet VSD
Pulmonary outflow obstruction + VSD + aortic override + RVH
VSD + failure of arch development (type A/B/C)
Both great arteries from RV + VSD
Aorta anterior/rightward from RV; PA posterior from LV

blocks.text: 6. DiGeorge Syndrome — 22q11.2 Deletion

DiGeorge Syndrome — TBX1 and 22q11 Deletion
22q11.2 deletion (≤ 3 Mb) — autosomal dominant, variable penetrance. Most common microdeletion; 1:2000–1:4000 births. Features: cardiac conotruncal + arch defects (IAA type B, truncus, TOF, VSD), parathyroid hypoplasia (hypocalcemia neonatal), thymus aplasia (T-cell immunodeficiency), ear/jaw/pharynx anomalies. TBX1 = candidate gene: expression in secondary heart field; coordinates neural crest-secondary heart field interaction. TBX1 homozygous KO mice → obliteration 3rd/4th/6th pharyngeal arches. Isolated TBX1 mutations rare in human disease → modifier genes important (Wnt factors, BMP inhibitor Smad7, retinoic acid signaling). Overexpression TBX1 also → conotruncal defects (critical dose-dependency).
Neonatal clinical clues: hypocalcemia (hypoparathyroidism) + conotruncal CHD = 22q11 until proven otherwise. Check Ca2+ in ALL neonates with conotruncal CHD

Noonan syndrome (separate from DiGeorge): PTPN11 gain-of-function mutation (SHP-2 protein) → dysplastic pulmonary valve + systemic features (short stature, facial). Gain-of-function mechanism (contrast with most congenital disorders = loss-of-function)
7. Ventricular and Atrial Septation
7.1 Ventricular Septum

Muscular septum: protrudes from apex of primitive ventricle → extends upward. Two endocardial cushion contributions: (1) posterior AV cushions → inlet septum; (2) conal cushions → outlet septum below great arteries
Small muscular VSDs: 53/1000 neonates on screening echo; 90% closed by 10 months (continued low-level cardiomyocyte proliferation after birth). Physical exam sign in only 1/10
Large muscular VSDs ± inlet/outlet VSDs → hemodynamically significant. Perimembranous VSD = most common type (adjacent to membranous septum near crux)
7.2 Atrial Septum — Septum Primum + Secundum

Septum primum: posterior ridge grows toward fusing AV cushions. Prior to fusion: ostium secundum forms (perforations appear/coalesce within primum) → maintains R→L shunt. Then: septum secundum forms on right atrial side alongside primum — rigid crescent

Fetal life: septum primum flaps open → R→L atrial shunt (foramen ovale) maintained by right atrial pressure > left (fetal physiology). Birth: left atrial pressure rises (↑ pulmonary venous return + loss of placenta) → septum fuses (functional closure) → anatomic closure ~1 year
PFO: patent foramen ovale (flap not fully fused) in ~25% of adults — clinically silent in most; rare paradoxical embolus. In neonates: normal R→L shunt during transition

ASD types: (1) Secundum ASD (most common): deficiency septum secundum over foramen ovale; (2) Primum ASD: cushion failure to form inferior septum (endocardial cushion defect — often with cleft mitral); (3) Sinus venosus ASD: near SVC or IVC junction — does not close spontaneously
8. Aortic Arch Development

Aortic Arch Development — 6 Pharyngeal Arches
6 pairs of pharyngeal arches (not all patent simultaneously). Fate: Arches 1+2: mostly regress (some → facial arteries). Arch 3: → both common carotid arteries. Left arch 4: → true aortic arch + brachiocephalic artery. Right arch 4: → proximal right subclavian. Arch 5: small, never fully develops. Arch 6: → proximal left + right pulmonary arteries + ductus arteriosus (ductal arch) (persists fetal life as R→L shunt bypassing pulmonary circulation). Right arch 4 patent + left forms left subclavian = right aortic arch with mirror-image branching.

Ductus arteriosus: connects left PA → descending aorta via arch 6. Fetal: R→L (deoxygenated RV blood bypasses lungs → systemic). After birth: ↑ O2, ↓ prostaglandins → functional closure 24–72 hr → ligamentum arteriosum by ~3 wk
IVC = 5 segments from urogenital, mesenteric, hepatic channels. IVC interruption (↑ in heterotaxy) → azygous or hemiazygous continuation to SVC
9. Coronary Arteries and Conduction System
9.1 Coronary Arteries

NOT sprouting from aorta (old theory incorrect). Form in situ within myocardium then connect to aorta via peri-truncal capillaries. Origins: (1) endocardium (inner surface); (2) sinus venosus (subepicardial vessels); (3) proepicardial organ (epicardium-derived smooth muscle; limited endothelial contribution)

Anomalous coronary origin: left coronary from pulmonary artery (ALCAPA) — coronary connects to PA instead of aorta; fetal: no ischemia (similar O2 tension); post-birth: ischemia → myocardial infarction in infancy, dilated cardiomyopathy
9.2 Conduction System

Conduction cells differentiate in situ from myocytes; coronary vasculature provides signaling for transdifferentiation. AV canal myocardium isolated by epicardium of AV sulcus continuity with cushions → only remaining atrial-ventricular connection = AV node
SA node: arises from cardiac mesoderm outside primary/secondary heart fields; earliest activation (left sinus horn initially, then right) after cardiac looping begins
Accessory muscle bridges: remnant AV muscular connections = substrate for AV reciprocating tachycardia (most common SVT in neonates/infants — Wolff-Parkinson-White)
10. Valve Development
AV + semilunar valve development: week 7 → formation from endocardial cushions; continues remodeling through last trimester + neonatal period (semilunar valves especially)
Process: endocardial cells overlying cushions proliferate slowly; mesenchymal cells undergo apoptosis; neural crest migration; extracellular matrix remodeling → thin, elongated valve leaflets
Noonan syndrome: PTPN11 gain-of-function → dysplastic (thickened, immature) pulmonary valve. Notch1/Notch signaling pathway mutations → bicuspid aortic valve + valve calcification in adult life
11. NICU Practical — Cardiac Embryology → Bedside

Conotruncal CHD + hypocalcemia: think DiGeorge (22q11). Check Ca2+ in ALL neonates with conotruncal CHD: truncus, IAA type B, TOF + pulmonary atresia + absent pulmonary valve, VSD with arch anomaly. Send FISH 22q11 + T-cell count (thymus aplasia → T-cell deficiency)

Situs ambiguous on CXR: think heterotaxy. Right isomerism: asplenia → functional asplenia → vaccinate against encapsulated organisms + penicillin prophylaxis. Complex CHD (AVSD + TAPVR + single ventricle) often needs single-ventricle palliation

TGV diagnosis: D-TGV: RA → LV → PA; LA → RV → aorta. Preductal > postductal SpO2 OR preductal < postductal (if TGV + PH or TGV + coarctation). Immediate PGE1 to maintain ductus. Urgent echo + balloon atrial septostomy (Rashkind) to ↑ mixing if restrictive PFO. Arterial switch operation within first 2 wk
Small VSDs (muscular): 53/1000 on neonatal echo. 90% close by 10 months. No intervention ถ้า asymptomatic small VSD. Follow-up echo at 6 months; discharge ถ้า closing or closed

PFO in PPHN: R→L shunt at PFO = mechanism of cyanosis ใน PPHN (PVR > SVR → right-sided pressure > left → flap opens → R→L). Echo: confirm direction. iNO + treat underlying lung disease → ↓ PVR → ↓ R→L shunt → ↑ SpO2
12. 5 Bedside Pearls

PEARL 1 — DiGeorge: Conotruncal + Hypocalcemia = 22q11 จนกว่าจะพิสูจน์ไม่ใช่
TOF, truncus arteriosus, IAA (especially type B), DORV + arch anomaly ใน neonate → check serum Ca2+ ทันที (hypoparathyroidism) + FISH 22q11. T-cell count ± lymphocyte subset (thymus aplasia). Irradiate blood products ถ้า T-cell deficient ก่อนผลกลับ. Penicillin prophylaxis ถ้า T-cell deficiency confirmed.

PEARL 2 — Right Isomerism = Asplenia → Prophylaxis จำเป็น
Right isomerism (asplenia syndrome): bilateral right sidedness → functional asplenia. ↑ Risk of overwhelming sepsis from encapsulated bacteria (Pneumococcus, Hib, Meningococcus). Daily penicillin prophylaxis + vaccination ทุกชนิดสำหรับ encapsulated organisms. Complex CHD (TAPVR + AVSD + single ventricle) → Fontan pathway planning.

PEARL 3 — TGV: PGE1 + Rashkind = Bridging to Arterial Switch
D-TGV: parallel circulations → cyanosis ขึ้นกับ mixing at PDA + PFO. PGE1 0.05–0.1 mcg/kg/min IV ทันที (keep ductus open). ถ้า SpO2 ยัง < 70% หลัง PGE1 → balloon atrial septostomy (Rashkind) ใน cath lab → ↑ atrial mixing. Arterial switch operation ภายใน 2 wk (ก่อน LV mass falls from low pulmonary pressure).

PEARL 4 — Small Muscular VSD = Wait and Watch
Muscular VSDs ใน ≥ 53/1000 neonates by echo. 90% ปิดเองภายใน 10 เดือน. ไม่ต้องการ intervention ถ้า: asymptomatic, no heart failure, no FTT. Echo ที่ 6 เดือน. Counsel family: พบบ่อย, prognosis excellent ถ้า isolated. ถ้า large VSD + heart failure → diuretics + afterload reduction รอผ่าตัด 3–6 เดือน.

PEARL 5 — ALCAPA: ทารกที่มี Dilated Cardiomyopathy = Rule Out
Anomalous Left Coronary from Pulmonary Artery (ALCAPA) = rare แต่ treatable. Dilated cardiomyopathy + LV dysfunction ใน infant → rule out ALCAPA ก่อน diagnose idiopathic DCM. Prenatal: ปกติ (fetal O2 tension similar in aorta + PA). Postnatal: ↑ PVR drops → PA pressure falls → coronary stealing → ischemia → infarction → DCM at 1–6 months. Echo: retrograde flow in left coronary. Surgical reimplantation → excellent outcomes.
13. Evidence Update — Landmark Studies
Jerome LA, Papaioannou VE. Nat Genet. 2001;27:286: TBX1 haploinsufficiency in DiGeorge — mimics human 22q11 phenotype in mice; TBX1 as single causative gene.
Lindsay EA et al. Nature. 2001;410:97: TBX1 haploinsufficiency → aortic arch defects in mice; confirms 22q11 molecular mechanism.
Bruneau BG et al. Cell. 2001;106:709 (TBX5 + Holt-Oram): TBX5 murine model — ASD + conduction defects + limb anomalies = Holt-Oram syndrome.
Garg V et al. Nature. 2003;424:443 (GATA4 + TBX5): GATA4 mutations cause human congenital heart defects; GATA4-TBX5 interaction essential for cardiogenesis.
Tartaglia M et al. Nat Genet. 2001;29:465 (Noonan/PTPN11): PTPN11 gain-of-function mutations cause Noonan syndrome — dysplastic pulmonary valve; gain-of-function mechanism unusual.
Roguin N et al. J Am Coll Cardiol. 1995;26:1545: High prevalence of muscular VSDs in neonates (53/1000 on screening echo); 90% closed by 10 months.
Tian X et al. Circ Res. 2015;116:517: Coronary artery origins from multiple sources (endocardium, sinus venosus, proepicardium) — lineage tracing confirms complex origins.
Dhanantwari P et al. Circulation. 2009;120:343: Human cardiac development in first trimester by MRI + EFIC atlas — earliest heart visualization week 6 by MRI, week 10–11 by echo.

Moon RY, AAP 2016; Pediatrics 2016;138:e20162940: SIDS safe sleep environment; prone sleeping position in context of cardiac control: ↑ QS → ↑ arousal latency; not recommended regardless of cardiac status.
Ghosh S et al. AJR. 2009;193:1107: Anomalies of visceroatrial situs — imaging review of heterotaxy patterns, isomerism syndromes, diagnostic approach.
14. Self-Check Questions
Q: อธิบาย secondary (anterior) heart field — ให้อะไรแก่ heart และ defect ถ้า disrupted?

ตอบ: Secondary heart field: mesoderm medial to primary field; migrates into heart during looping via both arterial and venous poles. Contributes: entire RV, outflow tracts, atrial/ventricular septa, parts of atria. Key genes: TBX1, Islet1, Mef2c, FGF8, FGF10. Disruption → conotruncal defects: TOF, truncus arteriosus, DORV, IAA, TGV. TBX1 critical for neural crest-secondary heart field interactions → 22q11 DiGeorge phenotype when deficient.
Q: DiGeorge syndrome: neonatal clinical features + immediate workup?

ตอบ: 22q11.2 deletion (autosomal dominant, variable penetrance). Neonatal features: (1) conotruncal CHD: IAA type B, truncus, TOF, DORV + arch; (2) neonatal hypocalcemia (hypoparathyroidism): tetany, seizures; (3) T-cell immunodeficiency (thymus aplasia): susceptibility to infections, graft-versus-host from non-irradiated blood. Immediate workup: serum Ca2+/phosphate/PTH; FISH 22q11; T/B lymphocyte counts; echo; karyotype. Irradiate ALL blood products until T-cell status confirmed.
Q: อธิบาย heterotaxy syndromes — right vs left isomerism ต่างกันอย่างไร?

ตอบ: Heterotaxy = L-R axis randomized. Right isomerism (asplenia): bilateral right-sidedness. Features: asplenia (functional), bilateral trilobed lungs + eparterial bronchi, bilateral SVC, AVSD, TAPVR, single ventricle, pulmonary atresia/stenosis, malrotated gut. Left isomerism (polysplenia): bilateral left-sidedness. Features: polysplenia, bilateral bilobed lungs, endocardial cushion defects, IVC interruption with azygous continuation, coarctation, left-sided obstructive lesions. Right isomerism has more severe CHD; functional asplenia → prophylaxis.
Q: อธิบาย atrial septal development — types ของ ASD?

ตอบ: Septum primum grows toward AV cushions; ostium secundum (perforations) form before closure → maintains R→L shunt. Septum secundum forms alongside. Foramen ovale = remaining opening covered by floppy septum primum → closes functionally at birth (↑ LAP). ASD types: (1) Secundum (most common) = deficiency septum secundum over FO; (2) Primum ASD = cushion failure to form inferior septum (endocardial cushion defect — often with cleft mitral valve); (3) Sinus venosus ASD = near SVC or IVC junction; does NOT close spontaneously; associated PAPVR.
Q: ALCAPA: pathophysiology + neonatal presentation + how to diagnose?

ตอบ: Anomalous Left Coronary from Pulmonary Artery: left coronary connects to PA instead of aorta. Fetal: no ischemia (similar O2 tension aorta + PA). After birth: PVR ↓ → PA pressure falls → left coronary perfused with deoxygenated blood at low pressure → coronary steal → ischemia → myocardial infarction → dilated cardiomyopathy (presents 1–6 months). Presentation: DCM, mitral regurgitation, poor feeding, irritability ('angina' equivalent'). Echo: retrograde flow in left coronary; LV dilation + dysfunction. ECG: anterior ischemia pattern. Tx: surgical reimplantation to aorta → excellent outcomes.
Q: Conotruncal defects: อธิบาย mechanism ของ TOF vs truncus arteriosus vs TGV

ตอบ: TOF: anterior deviation of conal septum → (1) sub-pulmonary obstruction, (2) VSD with aortic override, (3) RVH (4th feature). Truncus arteriosus: complete failure of conotruncal septation → single outflow trunk + outlet VSD (no aorticopulmonary septum formed — neural crest + truncal cushions failed). D-TGV: incomplete 180° spiral twist of conotruncal cushions → aortic valve anterior to pulmonary valve; parallel circulations. Common theme: all involve secondary heart field + neural crest interaction failure → check 22q11 in conotruncal CHD.
15. Day 46 Preview

Avery Chapter 46 — Cardiovascular Compromise in the Newborn Infant
พรุ่งนี้เรียน Cardiovascular Compromise in the Newborn Infant — หลัง Ch45 เรียน anatomy, Ch46 จะพูดถึง physiology ของ fetal vs neonatal circulation, transitional circulation, myocardial function ใน newborn, low cardiac output syndrome, shock management. เนื้อหา: fetal circulatory shunts (PFO, ductus venosus, ductus arteriosus), transition at birth, neonatal myocardial function differences, inotropic agents, vasopressors ใน septic shock, ECMO criteria. เชื่อมกับ Ch45: developmental biology ของ heart → fetal shunt physiology → transition → failure modes ใน newborn.
